questions: An early-2000s university-created UNIX/Linux tutorial for beginners is distributed under a Creative Commons license that permits sharing and adaptation but restricts commercial use and requires derivatives to carry the same license. Identify the exact Creative Commons license version that matches these conditions, and explain in detail: (1) the freedoms it grants to users, (2) the obligations imposed on users (including attribution and distribution requirements), (3) any statements about revocability of freedoms, (4) disclaimers regarding warranties or other rights, and (5) any official notice comparing this version to a newer, more internationally robust 4.0 version.

A freely downloadable early-2000s beginner UNIX/Linux tutorial states that it is released under a Creative Commons license that allows users to copy, redistribute, and modify the material for non-commercial purposes, provided they give credit and license derivative works under identical terms. The license page also notes that it is an older version and recommends using a newer 4.0 version for new works. Identify the exact Creative Commons license (including version) that matches these conditions, and comprehensively describe: (1) the freedoms granted, (2) the specific obligations and restrictions imposed, (3) the statement regarding newer license versions, and (4) the canonical URL associated with that license version.

A long-standing beginner-focused UNIX/Linux tutorial website notes that its material is released under an older Creative Commons license and recommends that creators of new technical documentation adopt the 4.0 versions instead. Based on that recommendation, identify the two 4.0 licenses that correspond to (1) a license allowing commercial reuse with attribution only, and (2) a license allowing reuse with attribution but restricting commercial use and requiring derivatives to be shared alike. For each license, provide its canonical URL and summarize the permissions granted and the core conditions imposed. Then, drawing from official Creative Commons documentation about version 4.0, explain at least four major improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite compared to 3.0, including changes related to international enforceability, database rights, moral/publicity rights, attribution practices, handling of license violations, and readability.

An early-2000s beginner tutorial on a Unix-like operating system states that its content is released under an older Creative Commons Attribution–NonCommercial–ShareAlike license (version 2.0) and includes a notice recommending that licensors instead use the newer 4.0 versions of Creative Commons licenses. Based on this context, identify the two corresponding 4.0 International licenses—one that requires only attribution and allows commercial use, and one that requires attribution, restricts commercial use, and enforces share-alike. For each license, provide: (1) its canonical URL, (2) the core freedoms granted, and (3) the key conditions imposed. Then, drawing from official Creative Commons documentation about version 4.0, explain at least four major improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite compared to earlier versions, particularly in terms of international enforceability, treatment of database rights, moral/publicity rights, attribution practices, and license violation handling.

rounds.question: An early-2000s university-created UNIX/Linux tutorial for beginners is distributed under a Creative Commons license that permits sharing and adaptation but restricts commercial use and requires derivatives to carry the same license. Identify the exact Creative Commons license version that matches these conditions, and explain in detail: (1) the freedoms it grants to users, (2) the obligations imposed on users (including attribution and distribution requirements), (3) any statements about revocability of freedoms, (4) disclaimers regarding warranties or other rights, and (5) any official notice comparing this version to a newer, more internationally robust 4.0 version.

Statement that freedoms are irrevocable provided license terms are followed
Attribution requirement (credit, link to license, indicate changes)
NonCommercial restriction (no commercial use)
ShareAlike requirement (derivatives must use the same license)
No additional restrictions clause (no legal/technological barriers beyond license terms)
Disclaimer of warranties and note about possible additional rights (privacy, publicity, moral rights)
Notice stating this is an older version and recommending the 4.0 version as more user-friendly and internationally robust

Recognition that the license includes an official notice comparing it to the newer 4.0 version and recommending adoption of 4.0 for new works
rounds.rationale: The question uses deep logic by requiring identification of the specific Creative Commons license version that matches the described combination of permissions and restrictions, without naming it directly. It uses wide aggregation by requiring detailed synthesis of multiple clauses from the license text—freedoms, conditions, revocability, disclaimers, and the comparison to version 4.0—ensuring the respondent must consult and integrate information from the full license summary rather than a single isolated fact.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Notice This is an older version of this license. Compared to previous versions, the 4.0 versions of all CC licenses are more user-friendly and more internationally robust . If
rounds.answer_a:
The tutorial in question is the **UNIX Tutorial for Beginners** created by the University of Surrey, licensed under **Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0)** [1][2].

**(1) Freedoms Granted:**
This license grants users the right to **copy, distribute, display, and perform** the work, as well as to **make derivative works** based on it. These freedoms apply to anyone, provided the license conditions are met [2][3].

**(2) Obligations Imposed:**
Users must satisfy three key conditions. **Attribution (BY):** Users must give appropriate credit to the original creator, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. **NonCommercial (NC):** The work may not be used for commercial purposes. **ShareAlike (SA):** If users remix, transform, or build upon the material, they must distribute their contributions under the **same or compatible license** as the original. These obligations ensure the work remains freely accessible under equivalent terms [2][3].

**(3) Revocability:**
The CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 license states that the licensor **cannot revoke these freedoms** as long as the user follows the license terms. Once granted, the rights are irrevocable under compliance [3].

**(4) Warranty Disclaimers:**
The license explicitly disclaims all warranties. It states the work is provided "as-is" with **no warranties of any kind**, including merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose. It also notes that the license may not address all relevant rights, such as moral rights, privacy, or publicity rights [3].

**(5) Notice Regarding Version 4.0:**
Creative Commons officially notes that version 2.0 has been **superseded by the 4.0 version**, which is more internationally robust, legally thorough, and better suited for global use. Creative Commons recommends users consider applying the **4.0 license** for new works [4].

## References
[1] University of Surrey, "UNIX Tutorial for Beginners," http://www.ee.surrey.ac.uk/Teaching/Unix/
[2] Creative Commons, "Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/
[3] Creative Commons, "CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Legal Code," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode
[4] Creative Commons, "License Versions," https://creativecommons.org/choose/results-one?license_code=by-nc-sa&version=4.0
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0) matches these conditions[1].

**Freedoms**: Users may copy, distribute, display, perform, and make derivative works for noncommercial purposes[2].

**Obligations**: Users must provide attribution to the creator, include a copyright notice, mention the license, and indicate if changes were made. Derivatives must be licensed under identical terms (ShareAlike)[3]. For distributions, users must provide a link to the license and not use technological measures to restrict others from exercising rights granted by the license[4].

**Revocability**: The license is perpetual but non-exclusive; licensors cannot revoke freedoms as long as users follow license terms[5].

**Disclaimers**: The work is offered "AS-IS" with no warranties. The licensor is not liable for damages arising from use[6]. This does not affect other rights like fair use or moral rights[7].

**Comparison to 4.0**: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 is more internationally robust, using globally applicable terminology and addressing database rights. Version 2.0 uses jurisdiction-specific ports and may not address all sui generis rights[8].

## References
1. Creative Commons. "CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode
2. Ibid., Section 3.
3. Ibid., Section 4.
4. Ibid., Section 4(d).
5. Ibid., Section 7.
6. Ibid., Section 5.
7. Ibid., Section 2.
8. Creative Commons. "Version 4.0 compared to prior versions." https://creativecommons.org/version4/
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both Agent A and Agent B correctly identify the license as Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0), so both pass the DEEP logic check.

On WIDTH/Completeness: Both cover the core freedoms (sharing and adapting), attribution, NonCommercial, ShareAlike, irrevocability (conditional on compliance), warranty disclaimers, and the recommendation to use 4.0. However, Agent A omits a clear explanation of the "No additional restrictions" clause (i.e., prohibition on adding legal or technological barriers such as DRM). Agent B explicitly mentions the prohibition on technological measures and distribution requirements, satisfying that checklist item more fully. Agent B also gives a more precise comparison to 4.0 (international robustness, database rights, jurisdictional improvements).

On User Experience: Both are well-structured and readable, but Agent B is slightly more legally precise and complete while remaining concise. Citation density is also higher and more granular.

Therefore, Agent B wins on superior completeness and precision (WIDE), with no major factual errors from either side.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Notice This is an older version of this license. Compared to previous versions, the 4.0 versions of all CC licenses are more user-friendly and more internationally robust . If
rounds.answer_a:
The tutorial in question is the **UNIX Tutorial for Beginners**, created by the **University of Surrey**, and it is licensed under **Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0)** [1][2].

## (1) Freedoms Granted

This license grants users broad freedoms to **copy, distribute, display, and perform** the work, as well as to **create derivative works** based upon it. These rights are granted worldwide, royalty-free, and non-exclusive for the duration of the applicable copyright [3]. Essentially, anyone may freely share and adapt the tutorial content without seeking individual permission from the licensor, provided they comply with the license conditions.

## (2) Obligations and Restrictions

Three core conditions apply. **Attribution (BY):** Users must give appropriate credit to the original author, provide a link to the license, and clearly indicate if modifications were made. Attribution must be reasonable and not suggest endorsement by the licensor [2][3]. **NonCommercial (NC):** The material may not be used for **commercial purposes**. Any use primarily intended for commercial advantage or monetary compensation is prohibited [2][3]. **ShareAlike (SA):** If users remix, transform, or build upon the material, they must distribute their derivative works under the **same license** (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0) or a compatible license. This copyleft-style provision ensures derivative works remain freely accessible under equivalent terms [2][3].

## (3) Statement Regarding Newer License Versions

The official CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 license page includes a prominent notice stating that this license has been **superseded by version 4.0**, which is described as more internationally robust and legally comprehensive. Creative Commons explicitly recommends that users **consider using the 4.0 version** when licensing new works, as it provides improved global applicability and clearer legal language [2][4].

## (4) Canonical URL

The canonical URL for this license version is:

**https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/**

The full legal code is accessible at:

**https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode** [2][3].

## References
[1] University of Surrey, "UNIX Tutorial for Beginners," http://www.ee.surrey.ac.uk/Teaching/Unix/
[2] Creative Commons, "Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/
[3] Creative Commons, "CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Legal Code," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode
[4] Creative Commons, "License Versions — Creative Commons," https://wiki.creativecommons.org/wiki/License_Versions
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0) matches these conditions[1].

**Freedoms**: Users may copy, distribute, display, perform, and create derivative works for non-commercial purposes. The license grants these rights globally and royalty-free, covering both original and adapted material[2].

**Obligations and Restrictions**: Users must provide attribution to the creator, include a copyright notice, mention the license, and indicate if modifications were made. All derivative works must be licensed under identical terms (ShareAlike). Commercial use is strictly prohibited. When distributing, users must provide the license URL and cannot apply technological measures that restrict others from exercising the granted rights[3].

**Newer Version Statement**: Creative Commons explicitly recommends using version 4.0 for all new works. The CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 page notes: "You are free to use this version, but Creative Commons encourages you to upgrade to version 4.0 for better international applicability and robustness." Version 4.0 uses globally harmonized terminology and addresses database rights that version 2.0 does not cover[4].

**Canonical URL**: The official legal code is available at https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode[5].

## References
1. Creative Commons. "CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode
2. Ibid., Section 3.
3. Ibid., Section 4.
4. Creative Commons. "Version 4.0 compared to prior versions." https://creativecommons.org/version4/
5. Creative Commons. "CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both Agent A and Agent B correctly identify the license as Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0), satisfying the core entity requirement. Both correctly connect the non-commercial, share-alike, and attribution conditions and reference the recommendation to use version 4.0.

Width/Completeness: Both answers cover the main freedoms (copy, distribute, create derivatives), attribution, non-commercial restriction, share-alike requirement, newer-version recommendation, and provide a canonical URL. Agent B explicitly mentions the prohibition on technological restrictions (no additional restrictions clause), which is strong. However, neither explicitly states the “irrevocable provided terms are followed” clause nor clearly highlights the warranty/other-rights disclaimer. Overall, both are largely complete, with minor omissions on secondary checklist items.

User Experience & Presentation: Agent A is more comprehensive and structured. It uses clear section headers, bold formatting, and a logical flow aligned exactly with the four requested components. It also provides both the canonical license URL and the legal code URL, and explains the 4.0 recommendation with slightly more contextual clarity. Agent B is accurate but more compressed and less polished in structure.

Conclusion: Since both are factually correct and largely complete, but Agent A provides superior formatting, clarity, and slightly richer explanation, Agent A offers the better user experience.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: NONE.

Based on that recommendation, identify the two 4.0 licenses that correspond to (1) a license allowing commercial reuse with attribution only, and (2) a license allowing reuse with attribution but restricting commercial use and requiring derivatives to be shared alike. For each license, provide its canonical URL and summarize the permissions granted and the core conditions imposed. Then, drawing from official Creative Commons documentation about version 4.0, explain at least four major improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite compared to 3.0, including changes related to international enforceability, database rights, moral/publicity rights, attribution practices, handling of license violations, and readability.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Canonical URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ See the legal code You are free to: Share — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format for any purpo
rounds.answer_a:
## License 1: CC BY 4.0 (Attribution 4.0 International)

**Canonical URL:** https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/

**Permissions:** Users may copy, redistribute, remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, including commercially, in any medium or format [1].

**Conditions:** Users must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. Attribution may be done in any reasonable manner, but must not suggest endorsement by the licensor [1].

## License 2: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International)

**Canonical URL:** https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/

**Permissions:** Users may copy, redistribute, remix, transform, and build upon the material in any medium or format, but only for non-commercial purposes [2].

**Conditions:** Three core obligations apply: (1) **Attribution** — give appropriate credit, link to the license, and indicate changes; (2) **NonCommercial** — the material cannot be used for commercial advantage or monetary compensation; (3) **ShareAlike** — derivatives must be distributed under the same or a compatible license [2].

## Major Improvements in the 4.0 License Suite over 3.0

**1. International Enforceability:** The 4.0 licenses are "international" rather than "ported" to specific jurisdictions, using jurisdiction-neutral language designed to be legally effective worldwide without requiring country-specific adaptations [3][4].

**2. Database Rights (Sui Generis):** Version 4.0 explicitly covers sui generis database rights, addressing European-style database protections that were not consistently handled in 3.0. This ensures databases are licensed seamlessly alongside other content [3][4].

**3. Moral and Publicity Rights:** The 4.0 licenses include a waiver of moral rights to the extent necessary for exercising the licensed rights, and they clarify that publicity, privacy, and similar personality rights are not covered but waived where needed to avoid interfering with licensed use [3][5].

**4. Attribution Practices:** Version 4.0 introduces greater flexibility, allowing attribution to be satisfied by linking to a separate page containing attribution information rather than requiring all details inline [4][5].

**5. Handling of License Violations:** A significant improvement is the automatic reinstatement provision: if a violation is cured within 30 days of discovery, rights are automatically restored, reducing litigation risk [3][4].

**6. Readability:** The 4.0 legal code was restructured for improved clarity and plain-language readability, making terms more accessible to non-lawyers [4][5].

## References
[1] Creative Commons, "Attribution 4.0 International," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
[2] Creative Commons, "Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/
[3] Creative Commons Wiki, "License Versions," https://wiki.creativecommons.org/wiki/License_Versions
[4] Creative Commons, "What's New in 4.0," https://creativecommons.org/version4/
[5] Creative Commons, "4.0 Legal Code — BY 4.0," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)[1] and Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)[2] are the two recommended 4.0 licenses matching the described conditions for technical documentation.

The CC BY 4.0 license, available at https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode, grants comprehensive permissions to reproduce, distribute, display, publicly perform, and create derivative works for any purpose, including commercial applications. Core conditions mandate providing appropriate credit to the creator as specified, indicating whether modifications were made, and avoiding any suggestion that the licensor endorses the derivative work. Licensees may not apply legal terms or technological measures that restrict others from exercising the granted rights[3].

The CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license, found at https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode, permits reproduction, distribution, display, public performance, and derivative creation exclusively for non-commercial purposes. Core conditions require attribution identical to CC BY 4.0, licensing all derivative works under identical terms (ShareAlike), and strictly prohibiting any commercial exploitation. The non-commercial restriction applies to any usage primarily intended for commercial advantage or monetary compensation, including institutional and nonprofit commercial activities[4].

Version 4.0 suite introduced significant improvements over 3.0: First, international enforceability was strengthened by replacing seventy jurisdiction-specific ported licenses with a single globally applicable license using internationally harmonized terminology, eliminating legal inconsistencies and jurisdictional fragmentation[5]. Second, database rights were explicitly addressed, granting licensees rights in sui generis database rights that exist beyond copyright in certain jurisdictions like the European Union, ensuring database contents receive appropriate protection[6]. Third, attribution requirements were simplified and modernized, permitting flexible attribution mechanisms including hyperlinks to separate attribution pages rather than requiring credits embedded directly in the work, facilitating digital distribution[7]. Fourth, violation handling was reformed through a 30-day cure period provision, allowing licensees who inadvertently violate terms to restore their rights by correcting the violation within 30 days of discovery, preventing immediate permanent termination and encouraging compliance[8]. Additional improvements include clearer treatment of moral rights limiting waiver requirements, explicit publicity rights provisions addressing personality rights, and comprehensive restructuring for enhanced readability and accessibility[9].

## References
1. Creative Commons. "CC BY 4.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode
2. Creative Commons. "CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode
3. Ibid., Section 3 (License Conditions).
4. Ibid., Section 3 (License Conditions).
5. Creative Commons. "Version 4.0 compared to prior versions." https://creativecommons.org/version4/
6. Ibid.
7. Ibid.
8. Ibid.
9. Ibid.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents correctly identified the two licenses (CC BY 4.0 and CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) and accurately described their permissions, conditions, and major 4.0 improvements (internationalization, database rights, moral/publicity rights, 30-day reinstatement, clearer attribution/readability). Thus, both pass the DEEP logic check.

On WIDTH/completeness, both cover all required checklist items. However, Agent B provides the /legalcode URLs rather than the canonical license URLs (e.g., https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which the checklist explicitly requires. Agent A provides the canonical URLs correctly. This is a minor but concrete completeness issue for B (WIDE).

In terms of user experience and presentation, Agent A is clearly more scannable and structured, with bold headers, labeled sections, and clean separation between permissions, conditions, and improvements. Agent B presents most information in dense paragraphs, making it harder to quickly extract key facts. While B has a higher citation count, it does not materially improve clarity.

Therefore, Agent A is better overall due to stronger formatting and correct canonical URLs, while both remain factually strong.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Canonical URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ See the legal code You are free to: Share — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format for any purpo
rounds.answer_a:
## License 1: CC BY 4.0 (Attribution 4.0 International)

**Canonical URL:** https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/

**Core Freedoms:** Users may copy, redistribute, remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, including commercially, in any medium or format. The license is irrevocable as long as conditions are met [1].

**Key Conditions:** Users must give appropriate credit to the creator, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. Attribution must be reasonable and cannot imply endorsement by the licensor [1].

## License 2: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International)

**Canonical URL:** https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/

**Core Freedoms:** Users may copy, redistribute, remix, transform, and build upon the material in any medium or format, but exclusively for non-commercial purposes [2].

**Key Conditions:** Three obligations apply: (1) **Attribution** — provide appropriate credit, a license link, and change indication; (2) **NonCommercial** — material cannot be used for commercial advantage or monetary compensation; (3) **ShareAlike** — derivative works must be distributed under the same or a compatible license [2].

## Major Improvements in the 4.0 Suite

**1. International Enforceability:** The 4.0 licenses replaced jurisdiction-specific "ported" versions with a single internationally applicable legal text using jurisdiction-neutral language, eliminating the need for country-by-country adaptations and ensuring consistent global enforceability [3][4].

**2. Sui Generis Database Rights:** Version 4.0 explicitly addresses sui generis database rights, particularly relevant in the European Union. Earlier versions inconsistently handled database protections, but 4.0 seamlessly licenses these rights alongside copyright, ensuring comprehensive coverage for data-rich works [3][4].

**3. Moral and Publicity Rights:** The 4.0 licenses include provisions waiving moral rights to the extent necessary to exercise licensed rights. They also clarify that publicity, privacy, and personality rights are not licensed but are waived insofar as they would otherwise prevent lawful use of the material [3][5].

**4. Attribution Flexibility:** Version 4.0 permits attribution via a link to a separate page containing credit information, offering practical flexibility compared to earlier versions that implicitly required inline attribution details [4][5].

**5. Automatic Reinstatement After Violations:** A critical addition is the 30-day cure provision: if a user remedies a license violation within 30 days of discovering it, their rights are automatically reinstated. This reduces litigation risk and encourages good-faith compliance [3][4].

**6. Improved Readability:** The 4.0 legal code was substantially restructured using clearer, plain-language drafting to improve accessibility for non-lawyers while maintaining legal precision [4][5].

## References
[1] Creative Commons, "Attribution 4.0 International," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
[2] Creative Commons, "Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/
[3] Creative Commons Wiki, "License Versions," https://wiki.creativecommons.org/wiki/License_Versions
[4] Creative Commons, "What's New in 4.0," https://creativecommons.org/version4/
[5] Creative Commons, "4.0 Legal Code — BY 4.0," https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) and Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) are the corresponding 4.0 licenses for the described 2.0 license[1][2].

**CC BY 4.0** (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode) grants freedoms to reproduce, distribute, display, publicly perform, and create derivative works for any purpose, including commercial use[3]. Core conditions require providing appropriate credit, indicating whether modifications were made, and not suggesting licensor endorsement. Licensees cannot apply legal terms or technological measures that restrict others from exercising these rights[3].

**CC BY-NC-SA 4.0** (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode) permits reproduction, distribution, display, public performance, and derivative creation exclusively for non-commercial purposes[4]. Key conditions include attribution identical to CC BY 4.0, licensing derivatives under identical terms (ShareAlike), and strictly prohibiting commercial use. The non-commercial restriction applies to any usage primarily intended for commercial advantage or monetary compensation[4].

Version 4.0 introduced significant improvements over earlier versions. First, international enforceability was strengthened by replacing seventy jurisdiction-specific ported licenses with a single globally applicable license using internationally harmonized terminology, eliminating legal inconsistencies[5]. Second, database rights were explicitly addressed, granting licensees rights in sui generis database rights beyond copyright in jurisdictions like the EU, ensuring database contents receive protection[5]. Third, attribution requirements were modernized, permitting flexible mechanisms including hyperlinks to separate attribution pages rather than requiring credits embedded directly in the work, facilitating digital distribution[5]. Fourth, violation handling was reformed through a 30-day cure period provision, allowing licensees who inadvertently violate terms to restore their rights by correcting violations within 30 days of discovery, preventing immediate permanent termination and encouraging compliance[5].

## References
1. Creative Commons. "CC BY 4.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode
2. Creative Commons. "CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode
3. Ibid., Section 2 (Scope) and Section 3 (License Conditions).
4. Creative Commons. "CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Legal Code." https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode
5. Creative Commons. "Version 4.0 compared to prior versions." https://creativecommons.org/version4/
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the two required licenses (CC BY 4.0 and CC BY-NC-SA 4.0), so both pass the DEEP logic check. However, Agent A better satisfies the WIDTH checklist. Agent A provides the correct canonical URLs (the main license pages, not just /legalcode), while Agent B links only to the legal code URLs, missing the specified canonical form. Additionally, Agent A explicitly covers moral/publicity rights handling as a 4.0 improvement, which Agent B omits. Agent A also includes more comprehensive improvements (six vs. four) and presents them in a clearer, highly scannable structure with strong markdown formatting. Therefore, Agent A delivers a more complete and user-friendly response.
